content: How does Paul address Timothy in the opening of 2 Timothy?
explanation: 2 Timothy 1:2 — 'To Timothy, my beloved child: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.'

content: According to 2 Timothy 1:5, in whom did sincere faith first dwell before Timothy?
explanation: 2 Timothy 1:5 — 'I am reminded of your sincere faith, a faith that dwelt first in your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice and now, I am sure, dwells in you as well.'

content: According to 2 Timothy 1:6, what does Paul urge Timothy to fan into flame?
explanation: 2 Timothy 1:6 — 'For this reason I remind you to fan into flame the gift of God, which is in you through the laying on of my hands.'
options: The gift of God received through Paul's laying on of hands

content: According to 2 Timothy 1:7, what spirit has God given us?
explanation: 2 Timothy 1:7 — 'For God gave us a spirit not of fear but of power and love and self-control.'
options: A spirit of power, love, and self-control

content: According to 2 Timothy 1:9, on what basis does God save us?
explanation: 2 Timothy 1:9 — '...who saved us and called us to a holy calling, not because of our works but because of his own purpose and grace, which he gave us in Christ Jesus before the ages began.'
options: His own purpose and grace given before the ages, not our works

content: According to 2 Timothy 1:10, what did Christ do to death and bring to light?
explanation: 2 Timothy 1:10 — '...our Savior Christ Jesus, who abolished death and brought life and immortality to light through the gospel.'
options: Delayed death and brought material blessings to light
Abolished death and brought life and immortality to light through the gospel
Restored the old order and brought the law to light
Reformed the world and brought wealth to light

content: According to 2 Timothy 2:2, to whom must Timothy entrust what he has heard?
explanation: 2 Timothy 2:2 — 'And what you have heard from me in the presence of many witnesses entrust to faithful men, who will be able to teach others also.' A four-generation principle of spiritual succession.
options: Only the children in the church
Faithful men who will be able to teach others

content: According to 2 Timothy 2:3, as whom must Timothy share in suffering?
explanation: 2 Timothy 2:3 — 'Share in suffering as a good soldier of Christ Jesus.'
options: A good soldier of Christ Jesus

content: According to 2 Timothy 2:8, what does Paul tell Timothy to remember about Jesus Christ?
explanation: 2 Timothy 2:8 — 'Remember Jesus Christ, risen from the dead, the offspring of David, as preached in my gospel.'
options: Risen from the dead, offspring of David
Performed the feeding of five thousand

content: According to 2 Timothy 2:9, though Paul is in chains, what about the word of God?
explanation: 2 Timothy 2:9 — '...for which I am suffering, bound with chains as a criminal. But the word of God is not bound!'
options: It is also imprisoned with him
It can only be passed orally

content: According to 2 Timothy 2:15, how must the unashamed worker handle the word of truth?
explanation: 2 Timothy 2:15 — 'Do your best to present yourself to God as one approved, a worker who has no need to be ashamed, rightly handling the word of truth.'
options: Rightly handle the word of truth

content: According to 2 Timothy 2:19, what are the two seals on God's firm foundation?
explanation: 2 Timothy 2:19 — '...God's firm foundation stands, bearing this seal: "The Lord knows those who are his," and, "Let everyone who names the name of the Lord depart from iniquity."'
options: 'The Lord knows those who are His' and 'Let everyone who names the name of the Lord depart from iniquity'

content: According to 2 Timothy 2:22, what must Timothy flee and what must he pursue?
explanation: 2 Timothy 2:22 — 'So flee youthful passions and pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace, along with those who call on the Lord from a pure heart.'
options: Flee youthful passions; pursue righteousness, faith, love, peace
Flee the church; pursue solitary life

content: According to 2 Timothy 3:1, what will come in the last days?
explanation: 2 Timothy 3:1 — 'But understand this, that in the last days there will come times of difficulty.'

content: According to 2 Timothy 3:12, who will be persecuted?
explanation: 2 Timothy 3:12 — 'Indeed, all who desire to live a godly life in Christ Jesus will be persecuted.'
options: All who desire to live a godly life in Christ Jesus
Only those guilty of grave sins

content: According to 2 Timothy 3:15, what can the Scripture do for Timothy?
explanation: 2 Timothy 3:15 — '...from childhood you have been acquainted with the sacred writings, which are able to make you wise for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.'
options: Make you wise for salvation through faith in Christ

content: According to 2 Timothy 3:16, what is the source of all Scripture?
explanation: 2 Timothy 3:16 — 'All Scripture is breathed out by God and profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for training in righteousness.'

content: According to 2 Timothy 4:2, what does Paul charge Timothy regarding preaching?
explanation: 2 Timothy 4:2 — 'Preach the word; be ready in season and out of season; reprove, rebuke, and exhort, with complete patience and teaching.'
options: Preach the word, in season and out of season; reprove, rebuke, exhort with patience
tags: Preach in and out of season

content: In 2 Timothy 4:7, with what three statements does Paul summarize his life?
explanation: 2 Timothy 4:7 — 'I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith.'
options: I was rich, I was famous, I was honored
I have written, I have spoken, I have judged
I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith
I have urged, I have reproved, I have asked

content: According to 2 Timothy 4:8, what is laid up for Paul (and all who love His appearing)?
explanation: 2 Timothy 4:8 — 'Henceforth there is laid up for me the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous Judge, will award to me on that Day, and not only to me but also to all who have loved his appearing.'

content: According to 2 Timothy 4:10, why did Demas desert Paul?
explanation: 2 Timothy 4:10 — 'For Demas, in love with this present world, has deserted me and gone to Thessalonica.'
options: In love with this present world
tags: Demas in love with the world

content: According to 2 Timothy 3:2-5, what marks people in the last days? (select all that apply)
explanation: 2 Timothy 3:2-4 — '...people will be lovers of self, lovers of money, proud, arrogant, abusive... lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God.' Many negative marks; 'lovers of righteousness' contradicts the description.
options: Lovers of self, lovers of money
Lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God

content: According to 2 Timothy 2:11-13, what statements form the trustworthy saying? (select all that apply)
explanation: 2 Timothy 2:11-13 — 'If we have died with him, we will also live with him; if we endure, we will also reign with him; if we deny him, he also will deny us; if we are faithless, he remains faithful — for he cannot deny himself.' He REMAINS faithful, not 'becomes faithless.'
options: Died with Him → live with Him

content: According to 2 Timothy 3:16-17, for what is Scripture profitable? (select all that apply)
explanation: 2 Timothy 3:16-17 — '...profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for training in righteousness, that the man of God may be complete, equipped for every good work.' Four profits; 'entertainment' is not the purpose.

content: According to 2 Timothy 4:6-8, how does Paul sum up his life and final hope? (select all that apply)
explanation: 2 Timothy 4:6-8 — '...I am already being poured out as a drink offering, and the time of my departure has come. I have fought the good fight... laid up for me the crown of righteousness...' Three things; 'accumulated great wealth' contradicts Paul's life of devotion.
options: Being poured out as a drink offering, time of departure has come
Fought good fight, finished the race, kept the faith
